steps.title: Apply triangle inequality to all three side combinations
steps.content:
For sides 8, 13, and $x$ to form a triangle, the sum of any two sides must exceed the third side:
  • $8 + 13 > x$ gives $x < 21$
  • $8 + x > 13$ gives $x > 5$
  • $13 + x > 8$ gives $x > -5$ (automatically satisfied)

steps.title: Combine the constraints
steps.content: The variable $x$ must satisfy both $x > 5$ and $x < 21$ simultaneously. This means $5 < x < 21$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
